rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROBYN CHRISTINA LEE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, as nothing has been filed to address the following issues (initially e-mailed to counsel on 03/05/2025). The petition has been pending for more than 5 months without any reported activity since it was initially set for 03/12/2025: 1) The caption of the proposed order must begin with "Order for Final Distribution". See LRSF 14.35(I)(6)(b). 2) The decedent's US savings bonds must be appraised by the probate referee and: i) The final inventory and appraisal must be corrected. ii) If redeemed during administration, the accounting schedules must be revised to describe each redemption. 3) Has petitioner taken any IAEA actions reportable under CRC 7.250(a)? If petitioner sold the automobile without giving notice of proposed action, was the sale made under Probate Code sections 10537(b)(3) and 10259(a)(2)? 4) Allegations must be made as to whether notice has been given to all known and reasonably ascertainable creditors of the decedent as required by section 9050, and if no notice has been given, whether the petitioner has made reasonable effort to ascertain such creditors. See 10900(b)(1). 5) The first (and final) account period exceeded 2 years from the date of death. No court approval has been obtained or sought to exceed the 2-year period. See LRSF 14.34(A). 6) Total charges were incorrectly calculated. The correct total should be $155,583.79, in which case the accounting does not balance, as total charges as corrected do not match total credits. See section 1061(c). PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF GRABIEL RIVERA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The accounting was submitted by the petitioner, who appears to have acted as a limited conservator of the estate while neither the appointment order nor the underlying petition outlined any specific powers or duties authorized to the petitioner. See Probate Code sections 1821(j)(1)(A) and 1830(b). At the last hearing, Counsel reported to clear the issue; however, no request has been made. The Court to determine how best to proceed. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EUGENE ARMISTEAD CARTER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 09/15/2025. 1) A copy of the lost will must be attached to the proposed order. See Probate Code section 8223 & LRSF 14.20. 2) A hard copy of the lost will must be provided to the clerk for a will file to be created. See LRSF 14.20. 3) No proof of publication is on file. See LRSF 14.20 requiring the word "lost" (will) to be specifically mentioned. 4) No DE-121 form is on file. A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the form. See LRSF 14.20 requiring the word "lost" (will) to be specifically mentioned. 5) Direct notice must be given to Victoria Beuerle and Michael Carter, absent their signed directive tnat notice should be given to their attorney. See CRC 7.51(a)(2). Their own addresses must be provided by a verified allegation to supplement item 8 of the petition. 6) No Attachment 3(f)(3) was attached to the petition. 7) Item 4 of the petition was not checked. 8) Was the decedent competent until death? See section 6124.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CHRISTOPHER CURRY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION TO TERMINATE ESTATE (NO ASSETS)
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Need an authority under which the court can terminate the proceedings when the executor has received or marshaled the estate's properties, whether or not the total value is below $184,500. The petition claims that there are no assets requiring administration; however, it does not specify how the personal properties and the $100,000 equity in the real property are being managed. If the executor has marshaled and controlled the assets under the full IAEA using Letters of Testamentary, an Inventory and Appraisal must be filed, and a petition for final distribution with a waiver of account needs to be submitted for the court's approval. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MAKO UCHIDA ROVA REVOCABLE TRUST DATED 5/24/05
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order To Compel Dean Ito Taylor (I) To Turn Over All Trust Property And (Ii) Account
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/6/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The only correct form of notice for a trust petition is the mandatory Judicial Council form DE-120. The proof of service on the reverse side of that form should be used. If a pleading form of proof of service is used instead, a copy of the Notice of Hearing (+ remote instructions) that was served must be attached to the proof of service. (2) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 9/22/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KERN YEE WONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/22/25. Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The amended petition was not signed and verified by the Court-appointed fiduciary. [Probate Code Section 1023 and CRC 7.103(c).] (2) Notice is defective if mailed before the filing of the pleading requiring notice. [LRSF 14.14 A.] The amended petition was e-filed on 7/25/25. Notice of it (not an "amended notice" but a notice of "amended petition") should not have been served until 7/25/25 or later. (3) The amended report still does not fully meet the requirements of LRSF 14.35 J. (4) If Bryan Wong died on 1/14/20 (on "December 2019," as initially alleged in the petition filed on 11/6/23), he survived this decedent. Did he have a trust? Has a personal representative been appointed for his estate? (His minor daughter's interest is not represented by her mother unless the mother has been granted legal authority.) Please courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 10/8/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (The Order for Probate was filed on 2/14/24, not 2/16/24. The will was admitted on 2/14/24, not 2/16/24.)
